channels.label: CM's Office Grievance Cell Help Desk
channels.source_name: Grievance Cell, Chief Minister's Office, Government of West Bengal
channels.note: Published on the official CMO grievance portal as "Call: 82820 82820" and as the "Help Desk" number, repeated in the site footer under "Copyright Grievance Cell, Chief Minister's Office, West Bengal". Confirmed identically in both the English and Bengali versions of the site. IMPORTANT: this is NOT the widely-blogged 9137091370 ("Didi Ke Bolo"), which is a political-campaign number on a non-government domain (didikebolo.com) and was not usable as a source. Verified 2026-07-15 by reading the live site. The portal is a JavaScript app, so the number renders in-browser rather than in raw HTML.

channels.label: West Bengal CMO Public Grievance Portal (Raise / Track a Grievance)
channels.source_name: Chief Minister's Office, Government of West Bengal
channels.note: The state's main citizen grievance portal, run by the Programme Implementation & Grievance Cell, CMO. Offers "Raise Grievance" and "Know Your Grievance" (status tracking), in English and Bengali. Live (HTTP 200) as of 2026-07-15.

channels.label: West Bengal Police - official website
channels.source_name: West Bengal Police
channels.note: Official state police site. Hosts the Emergency Dialling page and the important-telephone-numbers directory (~1,254 district/PS contact records). Verified live 2026-07-15.

channels.label: West Bengal Police Citizen Services portal (CCTNS - 'Police Sohaiyota')
channels.source_name: West Bengal Police - Citizen Services (CCTNS)
channels.note: The state police citizen-services portal: FIR copy requests, procession permission, C-Form and other citizen services. Linked from wbpolice.gov.in. Verified live 2026-07-15.

channels.label: Police Control Room (West Bengal)
channels.source_name: West Bengal Police - Citizen Services (CCTNS)
channels.note: Published on the WB Police citizen-services portal explicitly as "Police Control Room - 100", and as "Dial 100" on the WB Police Emergency Dialling page (https://wbpolice.gov.in/wbp/Common/WBP_Emergency_Dial.aspx). This is the national police short code, but it is what WB Police itself publishes as its control room number. NOTE: the state portal also lists landlines "West Bengal Police: 033-22144031 / 1946" and "Lalbazar Control Room: 033-22143024", but that page is stamped "Last updated on: Saturday, 30th April 2022" and still names the long-dissolved "West Bengal State Electricity Board", so I did not treat those landlines as reliable enough to cite.

channels.label: Government of West Bengal official state portal ('Egiye Bangla')
channels.source_name: Government of West Bengal
channels.note: The official state portal, maintained by the Department of Information & Cultural Affairs. Verified live 2026-07-15.

channels.label: West Bengal district directory - District & Local Government
channels.source_name: Government of West Bengal - District and Local Government
channels.note: THE district directory you asked for. Live (HTTP 200) and links out to all 23 district websites on .gov.in/.nic.in (27 official outbound links total), e.g. north24parganas.gov.in, s24pgs.gov.in, bankura.gov.in, birbhum.gov.in, coochbehar.gov.in, ddinajpur.nic.in, darjeeling.gov.in, hooghly.nic.in, howrah.gov.in, jalpaiguri.gov.in, jhargram.gov.in, kalimpong.gov.in, malda.gov.in, murshidabad.gov.in, nadia.gov.in, paschimbardhaman.gov.in, purbabardhaman.nic.in, paschimmedinipur.gov.in, purbamedinipur.gov.in, purulia.gov.in, uttardinajpur.gov.in, alipurduar.gov.in; Kolkata points to the KMC portal. Caveat: page is stamped "Last updated on: Thursday, 9th Sept 2021", though the district links themselves still resolve. An alternative official listing is https://wb.gov.in/contact-directories.aspx.

channels.label: WBSEDCL electricity helpline (24x7) - West Bengal outside CESC's Kolkata-Howrah area
channels.source_name: West Bengal State Electricity Distribution Company Ltd (WBSEDCL) - Contact Us
channels.note: Published on WBSEDCL's official Contact Us page under "Helpline Number (24X7)", and on the WBSEDCL homepage as "Helpline No.: 19121" with a tel: link. WBSEDCL is the state DISCOM covering West Bengal outside the CESC (greater Kolkata) licence area. Verified 2026-07-15. DELIBERATELY OMITTED: the "West Bengal State Electricity Board: 033-23591915" number on the state portal's emergency page - WBSEB was dissolved in 2007 and that page was last updated in 2022, so I judged it unsafe to publish.

channels.label: WBSEDCL grievance redressal / lodge an electricity complaint
channels.source_name: WBSEDCL - Grievance Redressal
channels.note: Official WBSEDCL grievance page (Lodge Grievance, contact details, redressal timelines). It links to the no-login complaint form at https://portal.wbsedcl.in/irj/go/km/docs/bills/IFRAME/ComplaintRegistrationWithoutLogin.html, which I also confirmed returns HTTP 200. Verified 2026-07-15.

channels.label: CESC electricity helpline - Kolkata & Howrah licence area (24x7)
channels.source_name: CESC Limited - Connect With Us (official site)
channels.note: CESC Limited is the sole distribution licensee within 567 sq km of Kolkata, Howrah and adjoining areas (per cesc.co.in/aboutUs); WBSEDCL serves the rest of West Bengal. LT helpline numbers published: 1912, 1860 500 1912, 033 3501 1912, 033 4403 1912.

channels.label: CESC online complaint registration - Kolkata & Howrah
channels.source_name: CESC Limited - Complaints & Requests (official site)
channels.note: Official complaint registration for CESC consumers (billing/supply), with a separate quick link for reporting supply-off/no-power. Confirmed working 2026-07-16.

channels.label: CESC Limited head office / general contact email
channels.source_name: CESC Limited - Connect With Us

channels.label: National Emergency Number (ERSS) - Police, Fire & Rescue, Health
channels.source_name: Ministry of Home Affairs, Government of India
channels.note: MHA's single pan-India emergency number: 'ONE INDIA ONE EMERGENCY NUMBER 112', a nationwide unified emergency response system. Works in every State/UT. Corroborated by india.gov.in helpline directory, which lists 112 as the Integrated Helpline (ERSS) covering Police, Fire & Rescue and Health services.

channels.label: Fire Helpline
channels.source_name: National Portal of India (india.gov.in), NIC / MeitY
channels.note: Listed under 'Emergency Numbers' on the National Portal of India helpline directory (page last reviewed/updated 14 July 2026).

channels.label: National Ambulance Service (NAS)
channels.source_name: National Portal of India (india.gov.in), NIC / MeitY
channels.note: Listed as 'National Ambulance Service (NAS)' under Emergency Numbers on the National Portal of India helpline directory (page last reviewed/updated 14 July 2026).

channels.label: Women Helpline (national, toll-free)
channels.source_name: Ministry of Women and Child Development, Government of India
channels.note: Published as 'Women Helpline Toll free No. - 181' directly by the owning ministry (WCD) as a tel: link on its homepage. Also listed on india.gov.in as both 'Women Helpline Number' and 'Domestic Violence Helpline'.

channels.label: National Commission for Women (NCW) 24x7 Helpline
channels.source_name: National Commission for Women (NCW)
channels.note: NCW's own site states: 'Call the 24x7 NCW helpline at 14490 for immediate assistance and support', and lists 'NCW Women Helpline (14490)' under its Complaint Redressal cells. IMPORTANT: the india.gov.in helpline directory still lists 7827170170 as the 'National Commission for Women Helpline' - that number appears nowhere on ncw.gov.in (not even in raw HTML), so it appears stale and was deliberately omitted in favour of the department's own current number.

channels.label: Child Helpline (national, toll-free)
channels.source_name: Ministry of Women and Child Development, Government of India
channels.note: Published as 'Child Helpline Toll free No. - 1098' directly by the owning ministry (WCD) as a tel: link on its homepage. Also listed as 'Child Help Line' on india.gov.in.

channels.label: Elderline - National Helpline for Senior Citizens (NHSC)
channels.source_name: Department of Social Justice & Empowerment, Government of India
channels.note: Published as 'Elder Helpline: 14567' on the Department of Social Justice & Empowerment's own Elderline site, which the ministry homepage links to as the 'National Helpline for Senior Citizens (NHSC)'. india.gov.in additionally lists a landline 011-20893999 alongside 14567 for the Senior Citizens Helpline.

channels.label: National Consumer Helpline (NCH)
channels.source_name: National Portal of India (india.gov.in), NIC / MeitY
channels.note: Listed as 'National Consumer Helpline (NCH)' on the National Portal of India helpline directory (page last reviewed/updated 14 July 2026). Note: the NCH portal consumerhelpline.gov.in could not be reached at time of research (DNS resolves to an NIC address but the host refused connections / returned 504), so only the number is cited here - not the NCH portal URL.

channels.label: Cyber Crime Helpline (online financial fraud reporting)
channels.source_name: National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal, MHA / I4C
channels.note: Confirmed on the National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal homepage banner (MHA / Indian Cyber Crime Coordination Centre - I4C), which reads: 'To report online financial fraud, call 1930' and 'register your complaint at cybercrime.gov.in'. Independently corroborated by the india.gov.in helpline directory, which lists 1930 as the Cyber Crime Helpline.

channels.label: National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal (file a cybercrime complaint)
channels.source_name: National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal, MHA / I4C
channels.note: Verified live. Ministry of Home Affairs portal for reporting cybercrime; supports anonymous reporting, women/children-related crime, financial fraud and other cybercrime, plus complaint tracking. Nationwide.

channels.label: CPGRAMS - Centralised Public Grievance Redress and Monitoring System
channels.source_name: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances & Pensions, Government of India
channels.note: Verified live; this is the citizen-facing portal. The site states CPGRAMS is 'an online platform available to the citizens 24x7 to lodge their grievances to the public authorities on any subject related to service delivery... a single portal connected to all the Ministries/Departments of Government of India and States.' Supports tracking by registration ID and an appeal facility. Note: it does not cover RTI matters, subjudice/court matters, or religious matters.

channels.label: Central Vigilance Commission - lodge a corruption complaint
channels.source_name: Central Vigilance Commission (CVC), Government of India
channels.note: Verified live. CVC's Complaint Management System with a 'Citizen Login', complaint status tracking and a published complaint handling policy. Linked from cvc.gov.in as 'Lodge a complaint'. Covers corruption complaints against central government organisations.

channels.label: Relief Commissioner for Natural Calamities
channels.source_name: National Portal of India (india.gov.in), NIC / MeitY
channels.note: Listed on the National Portal of India helpline directory (page last reviewed/updated 14 July 2026).

channels.label: Road Accident Helpline
channels.source_name: National Portal of India (india.gov.in), NIC / MeitY
channels.note: Listed on the National Portal of India helpline directory (page last reviewed/updated 14 July 2026).
